Developing in One Sprint and Testing in the Next Is Not Scrum and it is causing some issues

When development and testing are split across two Sprints, stakeholders experience reduced visibility into the team's progress. They must wait longer to see what the Scrum team is working on, hindering collaboration and feedback. Furthermore, this approach decreases predictability as teams are effectively planning two Sprints' worth of work during Sprint Planning. Coordinating development and testing efforts across two Sprints can become burdensome, making it challenging to meet Sprint goals consistently.

Scrum's incremental approach grants teams the flexibility to change direction easily as they work on small, manageable increments of functionality. When development and testing are separated across two Sprints, the team accumulates a substantial amount of "half-baked" work. Consequently, it becomes challenging to pivot or respond to changing requirements swiftly. To change direction, the team must wait until both the development and testing phases are completed, which can take at least two Sprints.

The Agile Manifesto's very first principle emphasizes "early and continuous delivery of valuable software" as a key principle for Agile teams. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next introduces unnecessary delays in delivering value to the customer. If you are developing functionality in one Sprint and testing in the next, that means that it will take your Scrum Team at least two Sprint to deliver functionality to the customer, rather than one.

The 2020 Scrum Guide highlights that Scrum aims to control risk through an iterative, incremental approach. Developing in one Sprint and testing in the next increases the risk substantially. It postpones feedback from customers and stakeholders potentially leading to increased investment in the development of features that customers may not find useful. Additionally, it make take the Scrum team two Sprints - instead of one - to learn that planned functionality is not technically feasible. This can result in wasted effort and resources.

Separating development and testing into distinct Sprints introduces unnecessary complexity into the process. Teams must track which features have been developed but not tested beyond the scope of a single Sprint. Questions arise about whether separate product backlog items are needed for development and testing or how these items should be tied together. The lack of clear definitions can lead to confusion and inefficiency. It becomes difficult to gauge when development is truly complete.
Developing and Testing in the One Sprint will resolve these issues
Ideas on how we can achieve this
Feature Toggles: Use feature toggles to merge code into the main branch without impacting the functionality. This allows QA to test features even when they are not fully complete.
TDD/BDD: Adopt these practices to ensure that testing is an integral part of the development process, not an afterthought.
Test Automation: Automate repetitive and regression testing to save time and improve efficiency.
Deployment Automation: Automate the deployment process to facilitate smoother handovers between teams.
Cross-skilling: Encourage team members to learn skills across roles. This can include having developers learn basic testing techniques and QA team members understanding the development workflow.

Feature: Self-Signed Document Upload & Selection Objectives: To empower UAE PASS users with the ability to upload and select self-signed documents when official documents are unavailable.To streamline the document sharing process with service providers by incorporating user-uploaded documents into the sharing flows.To ensure continuity in services that require document verification when official documents issued by authorities are not available.Description: This feature enables UAE PASS users to upload self-signed documents and select them for sharing with service providers as part of the document sharing request process. It addresses scenarios where the necessary official documents are either not issued by the authorities or are unavailable due to specific document scope limitations. The feature aims to provide a flexible solution for document sharing by allowing users to upload a document of the required type or select an already uploaded document when proceeding with service requests.

Alternative Document Submission""Issued Document Replacement Sharing""Fallback Document Sharing""Supplementary Document Sharing""Uploaded Document Acceptance""Provider-Approved Document Upload""Substitute Document Sharing""Secondary Document Submission""Upload for Unavailable Issued Docs""Acceptable Uploads for Unissued Docs"

User-Facilitated Document Upload and Selection:Users must be able to upload documents directly to the app when an official document is not available.The system should allow users to select from previously uploaded documents for sharing requests.Service Provider Acceptance:The app must recognize and allow the submission of user-uploaded documents in scenarios where service providers accept such documents.  Integration and Reflection in User Interface:Changes made by users, such as document uploads or selections, should be accurately reflected within the app's user interface, specifically in the document sharing request screens.Support and Contact Accessibility:Users have access to support options, including contact information, if they encounter issues or have questions regarding document upload and selection.

"Upload and Share: Alternative for Unavailable Issued Documents""Service Provider-Accepted Uploads as Issued Document Substitutes""Substitute Sharing for Unissued Documents: Provider-Approved Uploads""Alternative Document Upload and Sharing for Unavailable Issuances""Provider-Endorsed Substitute for Unavailable Issued Document Sharing"

Integrating the second user story into the feature summary provides a comprehensive look at how UAE PASS can accommodate both one-to-one and one-to-many document scenarios where issued documents are unavailable. This addition ensures users can proceed with document sharing requests by uploading and selecting self-signed documents approved by the service provider. Feature Summary: Enhanced Document Upload and Selection for One-to-Many Document Cases Overview: This feature expands the capability for UAE PASS users to upload and select self-signed documents for instances where one-to-many issued documents are unavailable and accepted by the service provider, facilitating seamless document sharing requests. Acceptance Criteria: User Guidance for Missing Documents:When a required document is not found due to it not existing at the issuer side, a prompt "Couldn't find your document?" allows users to either select an existing uploaded document or upload a new one.A Contact Us option provides direct access to support through call or email for further assistance.Handling Document Scope Limitations:If a document is unavailable due to scope limitations, users are informed through a specific message. The option to upload or select a document remains available to ensure the user can fulfill the request.Unavailable Document Types:In scenarios where the document type does not exist in the digital vault, users are shown a status as per production, indicating no action can be taken for uploading a document. This ensures clarity and directs user actions appropriately.Predefined Cases for Specific Documents:For service provider requests targeting specific documents, users are provided with clear instructions to select or upload the necessary document type, enhancing the user experience by making document selection straightforward and efficient.Design Consideration: The design caters to both scenarios where users might need to navigate issues of unavailable documents due to issuer absence or scope limitations, including clear messaging and easy navigation for uploading or selecting alternative documents.By addressing both predefined and not predefined cases, the feature ensures users are never left without options, even when faced with document availability issues. This approach not only enhances user satisfaction but also streamlines the document sharing process, accommodating a wider range of user and service provider needs.

Overview:   As a UAE PASS user, I want to be able to upload and select a self-signed document instead of one-to-one issued document if the issued document is unavailable and it is accepted by the service provider within the sharing flows, so that I can proceed with document sharing request.   Acceptance Criteria:   Document does not exist at the issuer side, SP accept uploaded document, uploaded document type does exist in digital vault and uploaded document is available for selection If the document is not available at the issuer side and the service provider does accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does exist in digital vault, the following is displayed: Status will be "Unavailable | Please select or upload a document" Select button will replace request button, tapping on it will allow the user to select an uploaded document. After selection, document card details will change to an uploaded document card details as per the design If the user again decided to deselect the uploaded document, user can tap on it and go to the document details to deselect the uploaded document from the bottom of the screen. After confirming deselection, the document card will be reverted to the original state Document does not exist at the issuer due to document scope limitation, SP accept uploaded document, uploaded document type does exist in digital vault and uploaded document is available for selection If the document is not available at the issuer side due to document scope availability and the service provider does accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does exist in digital vault, the following is displayed: Status will be ""Unavailable | Please select or upload a self-signed version"{} A dropdown arrow will appear instead of the request button, without tapping on it will show the following: Error message as per the design Document scope message if applicable for the document (Please refer to DVT-1662 to understand which documents have document scope limitations) Select an uploaded document button: Tapping on it will allow the user to select an uploaded document After selection, document card details will change to an uploaded document card details as per the design If the user again decided to deselect the uploaded document, user can tap on it and go to the document details to deselect the uploaded document from the bottom of the screen. After confirming deselection, the document card will be reverted to the original state Document does not exist since it is unavailable at the issuer side or unavailable due to scope limitation, SP accept uploaded document and uploaded document type does not exists in digital vault If the document is not available at the issuer side or not available due to document scope availability and the service provider do accept uploaded document but the uploaded document type does not exists in digital vault, status will be as production and no option will be provided for the user to select an uploaded document. Handling generic error while trying to auto-assign uploaded document to the request for sharing screen  After completing the uploading and signing flow, and once user tap on "ok" button in the uploaded successful screen, if an error occurred while trying to auto assign the document to the request for sharing screen, make sure that the user is presented with a generic error message as per the design. If user tap on "ok" in the error pop-up, make sure that the user is redirected to the document selection screen.   Design:    DTP_for Review (2024 New) – Figma

Feature Summary: User-Initiated Document Upload and Self-Signing Objectives: Enhance user autonomy by enabling the upload of self-signed documents directly within the sharing flow.Expand service provider flexibility to accept user-submitted documents as part of their service criteria.  Description: This feature allows users to upload documents not automatically issued or available through the app, sign them electronically, and share them securely with service providers. It addresses gaps in the current document issuance system by empowering users to provide necessary documentation proactively. The feature includes a user-friendly interface for uploading, a secure method for electronic signing, and a straightforward process for document sharing while ensuring data protection and privacy compliance. Acceptance Criteria: Upload Functionality: Users can upload documents in specified formats (e.g., PDF, DOCX) with clear instructions on file size and type limitations.Self-Signing Mechanism: A secure and legally binding method for users to sign documents electronically is available.Verification Process: Uploaded and signed documents undergo a preliminary automated check for authenticity and completeness before being made available to service providers.Service Provider Integration: Service providers can easily access, review, and accept submitted documents through their existing interfaces with the application.User Consent and Privacy: Users are clearly informed about how their documents and data will be used, with explicit consent obtained for sharing with service providers.Security Standards: All documents are encrypted in transit and at rest, adhering to international data protection standards.Audit and Feedback: An audit trail for document upload, signing, and sharing activities is maintained, and users can report issues or provide feedback on the process.User and Provider Support: Documentation and support are available to guide both users and service providers through the new feature.

Secure Document Sharing: Users can generate a temporary, secure QR code within the app that grants access to specified documents. This QR code can be presented to service providers, who, upon scanning, gain temporary access to the documents without the need for physical copies. This method ensures that document sharing is both secure and efficient.

Document Verification at Points of Service: For in-person verification, users can present a QR code that, when scanned by a service provider, verifies the authenticity of the document in real-time from the issuing authority. This use case can be particularly useful in sectors requiring high levels of document integrity, such as healthcare, banking, and government services.

Interoperability with Other Government Services: Enable seamless integration with other government services by allowing users to scan QR codes at kiosks, government offices, or through other government apps to instantly access or submit required documents. This integration can facilitate a unified and efficient experience across government services.

Event and Facility Access: Use QR codes as digital passes for access to government-organized events or facilities. Users can request access through UAE Pass, and upon approval, receive a QR code as their entry ticket. This system not only simplifies access management but also enhances security by verifying the identity of attendees.

Overview:   As a UAE PASS user, I want to be able to easily request for unavailable documents within the sharing flows, so that I can add them and proceed with document sharing.   Acceptance Criteria:   -> One to many document mandatory for issued and optional cases (Not Predefined)   If the user land on selection screen and not currently in the digital vault, make sure a CTA button "Request" is displayed on the right side of the card as per the design for the following cases: One to many mandatory issued document - Unavailable document case One to many optional issued document - Unavailable document case In all cases, Tapping on "Request" will display a pop-up to confirm document request which contains: Document name Sentence: as per the design Information that will be used to request the document (i.e., Emirates ID number) Actions: Confirm request button, to confirm document request Cancel button to cancel document request Tapping on "Confirm request", will redirect the user to the issued document selection screen, where document card will display a progress bar instead of the document status. Once progress bar is completed, the progress bar will be replaced with document status as follows: Not currently in your digital vault -> Request failed and SP accept or do not accept uploaded document If the document request failed and the service provider do not accept uploaded document, the status will be "Request Failed". If the user leaves the document selection screen and revisited it, the document status will change from "request failed" to "not currently in your digital vault", which allows the user to try again. If user tap on the request failed document card, a pop up will be displayed, which contains the following: Title: Request Failed Message: As per the design Contact Us Action: tapping on it will display another pop-up which contains: Title: Contract us Call center number: 600561111, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it UAEPASS support email: Support@uaepass.ae, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it Ok Action: Tapping on it, will close the pop-up and take the user back to the selection screen If user tap on "Couldn't find your documents, a pop up will be displayed, which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Message: As per the design Contact Us Action: tapping on it will display another pop-up which contains: Title: Contract us Call center number: 600561111, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it UAEPASS support email: Support@uaepass.ae, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it Cancel Action: Tapping on it, will close the pop-up and take the user back to the selection screen Not currently in your digital vault -> Request Failed due to a generic error If an error occurred after tapping on "Confirm request", a pop-up message will appear in the middle of the screen to highlight the relevant error details. After tapping on "ok" in the error pop-up message, the user will return to the selection screen. If user tap on "Couldn't find your documents, a pop up will be displayed, which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Message: As per the design Contact Us Action: tapping on it will display another pop-up which contains: Title: Contract us Call center number: 600561111, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it UAEPASS support email: Support@uaepass.ae, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it Cancel Action: Tapping on it, will close the pop-up and take the user back to the selection screen Not currently in your digital vault -> Request Failed due issue in firestore in getting the latest status Make sure that there is a configured time is defined for issues in receiving firestore cloud updates.  Make sure that the initial configured time is set as per production. If the user request unavailable document and the progress bar is taking longer than the configured time due firestore cloud updates issue, make sure that the user is presented with pop-up error message which displays the following: Title: Processing In Progress Message: As per the design Refresh Action: Tapping try again will refresh the screen and the user will be able to request the document again.   If the user request another unavailable document while waiting for the first document, make sure that the configured waiting time resets and the pop-up will appear if the progress bar is taking longer than the configured time due firestore cloud updates issue.{} If user tap on "Couldn't find your documents, a pop up will be displayed, which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document?<br class="softbr

Overview:   As a UAE PASS user, I want to be able to understand why I didn't find the document i want to select in the document selection screen, so I'm aware of the reason and can act accordingly to continue with sharing the document with the service provider.   Acceptance Criteria:   Document does not exist at the issuer   If user access the document selection screen and was not able to find the document to be selected since the document does not exist at the issuer side, make sure that tapping on "Couldn't find your document?" will display a pop up which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Message: As per the design Contact Us Action: tapping on it will display another pop-up which contains: Title: Contract us Call center number: 600561111, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it UAEPASS support email: Support@uaepass.ae, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it Cancel Action: Tapping on it, will close the pop-up and take the user back to the selection screen{} Issue in getting the prefetch list from the issuer side   If user access the document selection screen and was not able to find the document to be selected since there is an issue in getting the prefetch list from the issuer side, make sure that a pop up is displayed which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Objectives: Enhance the document sharing flow in UAE PASS by clearly distinguishing between issued and user-uploaded documents for selection.Streamline user interface and experience to facilitate easier navigation and selection of documents for sharing with service providers, especially when navigating through unavailable documents.Description: This feature aims to refine the UAE PASS app's document selection process, introducing a segregated view for issued versus uploaded documents and improving the user interface for more intuitive navigation. It includes the implementation of prefetching documents for quicker access and introduces a new call-to-action for users to request documents that are not readily available, thereby improving efficiency and the overall user experience.

The enhanced feature introduces a fallback mechanism that significantly streamlines the document sharing process in UAE PASS. When documents are not up-to-date, users can now promptly trigger an update within the same workflow, eliminating the revocation error screen. This seamless approach not only expedites the sharing process but also avoids the disruption of restarting the flow, thereby improving efficiency and user experience during document sharing with service providers.

This feature enhances the UAE PASS by introducing document categories in the Document Tab for better organization, simplifying document addition for users, and enabling document requests and uploads directly from the homepage. It includes design updates for document scope in the revamp, allows requesting the same document type from different issuers, and improves the scrolling behavior on document tab screens to address clutter and enhance user experience. The update aims to streamline the process of requesting issued documents by focusing on document categories, significantly improving usability within the app.

Introduction and Background (5 minutes) Can you briefly walk us through your professional experience as a Business Analyst?Answer: "I’ve worked in the banking and retail industries for 8 years, leading cross-functional teams, gathering requirements, and delivering solutions that improved operational efficiency."What key projects have you worked on that are most relevant to this role?Answer: "One of my key projects was implementing an Agile framework for a digital transformation initiative, which streamlined processes and saved 20% in costs."Technical and Analytical Skills (10 minutes) How do you approach gathering and documenting business requirements? Can you share a specific example?Answer: "I use workshops, interviews, and process mapping. For example, in a CRM project, I facilitated sessions with sales and marketing teams to identify pain points and define requirements."What tools or frameworks have you used for business analysis?Answer: "I’ve used Jira and Confluence for Agile projects, Visio for process mapping, and UML diagrams for system modeling."How do you ensure that the requirements you gather align with business goals and stakeholder expectations?Answer: "I validate requirements through stakeholder reviews and traceability matrices to align them with project objectives."Describe a situation where you identified a problem that the stakeholders hadn’t noticed. How did you handle it?Answer: "In a project, I noticed inefficiencies in a legacy system that weren’t highlighted. I presented the findings with a cost-benefit analysis, and stakeholders approved a system upgrade."Behavioral and Soft Skills (10 minutes) Can you tell me about a time when you had to manage conflicting priorities among stakeholders? How did you resolve it?Answer: "In a recent project, marketing and IT had conflicting priorities. I facilitated a meeting to align their goals and proposed a phased approach that met both needs."Describe a challenging project you worked on. What made it challenging, and how did you handle it?Answer: "A project’s scope expanded mid-way. I conducted a change impact analysis and got stakeholder buy-in for an extended timeline."How do you collaborate with technical teams to ensure accurate implementation of business requirements?Answer: "I create detailed user stories and acceptance criteria, maintain open communication with developers, and participate in sprint reviews."What’s your approach to validating and testing that solutions meet business requirements?Answer: "I define test cases based on requirements, involve stakeholders in UAT, and conduct regression testing to ensure no issues arise post-deployment."Closing and Future Vision (5 minutes) What do you think are the most critical skills for a Senior Business Analyst, and how do you embody them?Answer: "Critical thinking, communication, and stakeholder management. I consistently apply these by breaking down complex problems and aligning teams toward shared goals."How do you stay updated with industry trends and new techniques in business analysis?Answer: "I attend webinars, follow BA communities, and recently completed a course on Agile business analysis."Do you have any questions for us about the role or the company?

Automated Draft Creation: Use AI to generate a first-draft proposal based on historical responses, reducing manual drafting time. Intelligent Data Extraction: Leverage NLP to extract key insights from past proposals and market data, feeding these into the AI for more informed outputs. Adaptive Template Generation: Develop dynamic templates that automatically adjust language, tone, and structure based on client requirements and industry specifics. Collaborative Input Aggregation: Create a platform that consolidates inputs from technical, design, and consulting teams into one unified draft. Real-Time Market Integration: Integrate live market trends and insights to ensure proposals are current and aligned with evolving client needs. Automated Gap Analysis: Implement AI to compare current RFP requirements with historical data, highlighting missing elements and suggesting improvements. Error Detection and Quality Control: Use additional AI layers (e.g., RAG) to verify the generated text against trusted data sources, reducing inaccuracies and hallucinations. Continuous Learning Feedback Loop: Build a system where human corrections and feedback are reintegrated into the model, ensuring ongoing improvement in proposal quality. Personalized Content Adaptation: Tailor proposal responses to reflect the unique tone and style of each client by fine-tuning the AI outputs. Efficiency Analytics: Deploy AI to track and analyze the success of past proposals, using that data to guide enhancements in future responses.

YES (High human effort without AI, Low validation effort) RFP Summary ToolReasoning: Manually summarizing lengthy proposals is time-consuming, but once the AI generates a summary, validation is relatively straightforward.Generative AI for Initial Draft of ProposalReasoning: Crafting a proposal draft from scratch is a high-effort task. If the AI’s output is fairly reliable, the human validation stage can be quick.MAYBE (High human effort without AI, High validation effort) Working Sample PrototypeReasoning: Building prototypes manually is labor-intensive. An AI-generated prototype requires careful testing and review, so validation remains high.UI/UX Prototype GeneratorReasoning: Designing user flows and interfaces can be time-consuming. An AI tool can speed up initial designs, but verifying usability and brand consistency demands significant human oversight.MAYBE (Low human effort without AI, Low validation effort) GenAI Agent to Capture Deadlines/UpdatesReasoning: Tracking deadlines is already a relatively low-effort task (often handled by calendars). Using AI further automates it, and validation is minimal (just a quick check for accuracy).Collaboration Generative AI for Team InputsReasoning: Gathering input from multiple teams is somewhat straightforward. AI can consolidate responses, and verifying that consolidation might be quick if each team checks their own contributions.NO (Low human effort without AI, High validation effort) AI Assistant for Capturing & Narrowing ScopeReasoning: Discussing scope with a client is relatively low-effort (human conversation), but if AI were used to interpret client requirements, it might need extensive review to avoid misinterpretation.Gap Analysis & Missing PointsReasoning: Identifying gaps can be a moderate task. But an AI incorrectly flagging or missing critical points might demand in-depth validation.Success Elements AnalysisReasoning: Analyzing reasons for success/failure can be done with minimal effort if records are well-kept. However, ensuring the AI’s findings are accurate could require heavy scrutiny.Quality Assurance LayerReasoning: Basic QA might be easy, but if the AI is double-checking specialized or legal aspects, verifying correctness is time-consuming.

On iOS, you generally don’t need an extra library just to handle “special” characters (like accented letters, Unicode symbols, etc.) in JSON. Apple’s built-in frameworks—namely JSONSerialization in Objective-C or Swift’s JSONDecoder—are designed to handle valid JSON payloads that contain any properly escaped or encoded Unicode characters. Using Apple’s Native JSON Parsing Objective-C (NSJSONSerialization) objc CopyEdit NSError *error = nil; id jsonObject = [NSJSONSerialization JSONObjectWithData:jsonData  options:0                                                   error:&error]; if (error) {     NSLog(@"Error parsing JSON: %@", error); } else {     NSLog(@"Parsed JSON: %@", jsonObject); } As long as the JSON data (jsonData) is valid and any “special” or non-ASCII characters are properly escaped (e.g., \uXXXX) or encoded in UTF-8, NSJSONSerialization can handle it. Swift (JSONDecoder) swift CopyEdit struct SomeModel: Codable {     let message: String     // other properties... }   do {     let model = try JSONDecoder().decode(SomeModel.self, from: jsonData)     print(model.message) } catch {     print("Error decoding JSON: \(error)") } Swift’s native JSONDecoder does a similar job: it decodes valid JSON into your Swift types, automatically handling any properly encoded Unicode or special characters. Common Third-Party Libraries You might come across popular third-party libraries such as SwiftyJSON or ObjectMapper. These libraries offer convenience APIs for working with JSON objects (like safer dictionary/array lookups), but under the hood they still rely on Apple’s native JSON parsing. They don’t have any special advantage in terms of “handling special characters”; they mainly provide a more streamlined or “Swifty” API. Example with SwiftyJSON: swift CopyEdit import SwiftyJSON   let json = try! JSON(data: jsonData) if let message = json["message"].string {     print(message) } Again, if the JSON is valid and special characters are properly escaped or encoded in UTF-8, this should “just work.” Potential Issues with Special Characters Invalid JSON: If the JSON you receive is not valid (for example, if it contains unescaped quotes or backslashes), no standard parser will handle it without errors. You might need to preprocess or sanitize the string before parsing.Encoding Mismatch: Ensure the server is sending UTF-8 (or another supported encoding) and that you’re reading it as UTF-8 on the client side. Mismatched encodings can result in garbled characters or parse failures.Bottom Line Native Approach: Use Apple’s JSONSerialization or Swift’s JSONDecoder—they handle all valid JSON, including special characters.Third-Party Libraries: Useful for convenience, but they don’t magically fix malformed or incorrectly encoded JSON.If You Still See Errors: Check that the server is escaping special characters properly and/or sending the data in the correct encoding (usually UTF-8).In most cases, no additional library is necessary—iOS’s built-in JSON handling is more than capable of dealing with special characters in well-formed JSON.

Opportunity: "Advance local public-private service interaction through an innovative AI chatbot. This chatbot, in harmony with a national digital vault and a cohesive digital platform, promises unparalleled task efficiency and enhanced user experience."

Idea: In the evolving digital landscape, local governance and private service offerings present vast opportunities. Yet, many users grapple with navigating these services efficiently. We advocate for a transformative solution that simplifies interactions, profoundly improving the user experience. We present an AI-enhanced local government service chatbot. This dynamic tool proactively discerns user needs and facilitates tasks, from obtaining licenses to processing government fee payments. Our chatbot's distinctive edge lies in its alignment with a national digital vault – a secure storehouse for official documents. Leveraging an authentication application, the chatbot confirms user identities and gains their explicit consent. This streamlined approach fetches necessary documents for service requests, eliminating cumbersome manual procedures. Moreover, it informs users of additional document requirements, ensuring smooth service applications. Furthermore, integrating our chatbot with a unified digital platform consolidates all local government services. Users can effortlessly access myriad services through one chat interface, a testament to its efficiency and convenience. In conclusion, our solution seeks to redefine digital governance locally. Prioritizing task efficiency and robust user identity protection, our proposition stands as a beacon for harnessing AI in public and private sectors. We believe our model combines speed, security, and efficiency, setting a precedent for nations globally.

The unique advantage of our chatbot is its integration with a national digital vault, a safe repository for official papers. The chatbot checks user identities and obtains their express agreement by utilizing an authentication program. This simplified method retrieves the required paperwork for service requests, doing away with laborious manual processes. Additionally, it alerts customers to any extra document needs, providing efficient service applications.   Additionally, combining our chatbot with a single digital platform consolidates all services provided by local governments. One chat interface allows users to easily access a variety of services, which is a sign of its effectiveness and practicality. Finally, our solution aims to reimagine local digital government. Opportunity: Revolutionize UAE public-private service access through a seamless, automated AI chatbot integrated with UAE Pass digital vault and unified digital portal, simplifying tasks and improving user experience."           Idea: "The United Arab Emirates is a country with enormous digital potential, leading the way in terms of governance and private business offers. However, accessing the multitude of various government and private services can be confusing and time-consuming for many inhabitants. We see an opportunity in reinventing how residents connect with these services, simplifying the process and improving the overall user experience. Our plan is to create an AI-powered government service enablement chatbot, a sophisticated digital assistant capable of conversing with users, understanding their needs, and assisting them in requesting any government or private services such as renewing vehicle insurance, applying for permits, scheduling appointments, and much more.   The novelty of our idea lies in its integration with the     Pass digital vault - a secure digital repository for official documents. Our chatbot would have access to this vault, enabling it to identify and use necessary documents for any given service application, thus eliminating the manual searching and uploading process for the user. The chatbot could also request additional necessary documents, thus making the application process smoother and more efficient. The chatbot would also function as a proactive digital assistant, following up on pending requests and updating the user about the status of their applications or services.   Another crucial aspect of our proposal is integrating this AI chatbot with the unified digital platform, a government initiative that aims to provide all government services digitally through a single portal. This integration would create a central hub where users can access and apply for various services through a single chat interface, increasing convenience and efficiency. We envision our solution to become a game-changer in the digital governance landscape, improving the way services are accessed, and saving valuable time for UAE residents. Furthermore, it would set a standard for other nations to follow, demonstrating how AI can be harnessed to improve public and private sector service delivery in a digital age."

It promises streamlined service access, a reduction in manual processes, and safeguards user identity, aiming to set an international benchmark. Opportunity: "Advance local public-private service interaction through an innovative AI chatbot. This chatbot, in harmony with a national digital vault and a cohesive digital platform, promises unparalleled task efficiency and enhanced user experience."

Idea: As nations embrace the digital age, there exists immense potential in enhancing governance and private service offerings locally. However, a challenge commonly faced by users is the intricacies and prolonged processes of navigating myriad local services. The opportunity we spotlight is in transforming these user interactions, making them more intuitive and significantly improving the overall user experience. Our proposition revolves around the conceptualization of an AI-powered local government service enablement chatbot. This digital assistant, adept in human-like interactions, is designed to decipher user needs and assist them in varied tasks, whether it's acquiring licenses, scheduling official appointments, or other essential services. A distinguishing aspect of our proposal is the chatbot's synergistic integration with a national digital vault – a fortified repository for citizens' official documents. Before accessing any information, the chatbot will utilize an authentication application to confirm the user's identity and obtain explicit consent. This not only ensures a secure experience but also streamlines the process by pulling necessary documents for any service request, thus eradicating the tedious manual search and upload procedures. Additionally, the chatbot is equipped to notify users if additional documents are needed, optimizing service application steps. Further enhancing its capabilities, our AI chatbot will be flawlessly merged with a unified digital platform. This platform envisions consolidating all local government services under one digital umbrella, ensuring users can access and apply for a myriad of services through a single chat interface, enhancing user convenience and operational efficiency. In essence, our groundbreaking solution aims to redefine the landscape of digital governance at a local level in any nation. It emphasizes streamlining service access, minimizing manual interventions, and fortifying user identity security, with the aspiration to establish a benchmark in how AI can profoundly influence both public and private service sectors, offering citizens an amalgamation of efficiency, speed, and security.

Idea: In the evolving digital landscape, local governance and private service offerings present vast opportunities. Yet, many users grapple with navigating these services efficiently. We advocate for a transformative solution that simplifies interactions, profoundly improving the user experience. We present an AI-enhanced local government service chatbot. This dynamic tool proactively discerns user needs and facilitates tasks, from obtaining licenses to processing government fee payments. Our chatbot's distinctive edge lies in its alignment with a national digital vault – a secure storehouse for official documents. Leveraging an authentication application, the chatbot confirms user identities and gains their explicit consent. This streamlined approach fetches necessary documents for service requests, eliminating cumbersome manual procedures. Moreover, it informs users of additional document requirements, ensuring smooth service applications. Furthermore, integrating our chatbot with a unified digital platform consolidates all local government services. Users can effortlessly access myriad services through one chat interface, a testament to its efficiency and convenience. In conclusion, our solution seeks to redefine digital governance locally. Prioritizing task efficiency and robust user identity protection, our proposition stands as a beacon for harnessing AI in public and private sectors. We believe our model combines speed, security, and efficiency, setting a precedent for nations globally.

Suggested Segregation of Actions into Stages for Sharing Journey Revamp To enhance the UX, the user actions can be grouped into stages based on logical flow and user intent. This segregation simplifies navigation, reduces cognitive load, and aligns with typical user journeys.   Stage 1: Notification and Awareness Receives notification Sees notification list Opens sharing request Justification:   This stage focuses on informing the user and drawing their attention to the sharing request. It ensures users are aware of actions needed, and the UX can be enhanced by providing clear, actionable notifications that lead seamlessly to the sharing request.   Stage 2: Review and Preparation Views sharing request details Requests missing documents Uploads missing documents Selects target document (one to many) Justification:   This stage is centered on preparing the user to fulfill the sharing request. Actions here involve reviewing details and ensuring the required documents are ready. Grouping these actions emphasizes clarity and completion, allowing users to focus on document preparation without being overwhelmed by subsequent steps. Stage 3: Consent and Authorization Provides Consent Triggers Sharing Request Enter PIN screen Justification:   This stage focuses on obtaining the user's consent and securely authorizing the sharing process. Grouping these actions ensures a smooth and secure flow, reducing user anxiety by explicitly signaling the transition to sensitive operations like consent and PIN entry. Stage 4: Completion and Feedback Sees sharing request result Justification:   This stage concludes the journey with feedback on the result of the sharing request. A dedicated completion stage reassures users that the process is finished and provides clarity on outcomes (success or failure), enhancing trust in the system. UX Enhancements for Each Stage Notification and Awareness:   Simplified notifications: Use concise, action-driven messages. Quick access: Ensure tapping the notification directly leads to the relevant sharing request. Review and Preparation:   Progress indicator: Show users how many documents are prepared/missing. Guided flows: Provide in-app guidance for document uploads and selection. Consent and Authorization:   Clear consent language: Highlight what the user is consenting to in simple terms. Streamlined PIN entry: Optimize for biometric fallback where available. Completion and Feedback:   Result clarity: Use visuals (e.g., checkmarks or warnings) to indicate success or failure. Next steps: Offer clear guidance on what users can do after completing the request (e.g., view document or start a new request). This grouping and UX enhancements simplify the journey, align with user expectations, and create a logical and intuitive flow, ultimately boosting satisfaction and minimizing confusion.

Overview:   As a UAE PASS user, I want to be able to upload and select a self-signed document instead of one-to-many issued documents if the issued document is unavailable and it is accepted by the service provider within the sharing flows, so that I can proceed with document sharing request.   Acceptance Criteria:   Not predefined Cases   Document does not exist at the issuer, service provider accepts uploaded documents and uploaded document type exists in digital vault    If user access the document selection screen and was not able to find the document to be selected since the document does not exist at the issuer side, make sure that tapping on "Couldn't find your document?" will display a pop up which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Message: As per the design Select or upload a document action: tapping on it will redirect the user to the uploaded document selection screen, where user will select uploaded document of that type or upload a new document if no document is available. Once selection is confirmed, the document will be reflected in the request for sharing screen. Contact Us Action: tapping on it will display another pop-up which contains: Title: Contract us Call center number: 600561111, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it UAEPASS support email: Support@uaepass.ae, production behavior will be followed if user tap on it Cancel Action: Tapping on it, will close the pop-up and take the user back to the selection screen.   Document does not exist digitally at the issuer due to document scope. service provider accepts uploaded documents and uploaded document type exists in digital vault{}   If user access the document selection screen and was not able to find the document to be selected since the document does not exist digitally at the issuer side due document scope limitation, make sure that tapping on "Couldn't find your document?" will display a pop up which contains the following: Title: Couldn't find your document? Message one: As per the design Message two: Document scope message if it exists for the document type.  Select or upload a document action: tapping on it will redirect the user to the uploaded document selection screen, where user will select uploaded document of that type or upload a new document if no document is available. Once selection is confirmed, the document will be reflected in the request for sharing screen. bjective: Enhance the document management experience within the UAE PASS app by updating the UI for uploaded documents, introducing new functionalities for document viewing and sharing, and streamlining the document update process. Description: This feature aims to refine the uploaded documents interface, adding detailed views and direct actions for PDF sharing and viewing. The technical enhancement involves decoupling REDIS from the application and running it as an independent service. This separation allows for independent updates of REDIS and its libraries without impacting the application. The process includes installing JAVA 17 and updated Spring Boot + JEDIS on a separate RHEL 8+ server, which acts as middleware for REDIS access. The DTP codebase will be updated to interact with this new middleware through internal API calls, enhancing the application's flexibility and maintainability.

This feature streamlines the process of securing digital credentials by fetching a visualization PDF and the verifiable credential JSON from the issuer, compressing and embedding the latter into the PDF as metadata. It further enhances security by adding a PAdES signature to the PDF, making it tamper-proof. Users can then download this secure document. For verification, UAEVERIFY checks the PDF's PAdES signature and the embedded metadata against the DTP Blockchain, ensuring the document's authenticity and integrity.

It adds toast messages to guide users, a countdown timer for sharing request expiry on service provider cards, and incorporates entity logos for better visual identification of documents. Objectives: Enhance UAE PASS by introducing QR code-based peer-to-peer document sharing.Facilitate easy and secure document exchange between users both online and in-person.Increase user control over shared document permissions.Boost awareness and adoption of the document sharing feature within the app.Description: This feature revolutionizes document sharing in UAE PASS, allowing users to scan a QR code to initiate peer-to-peer sharing requests. It supports sharing through multiple channels and enables specific permissions for each shared document, significantly improving the ease and security of document exchanges and encouraging more frequent use of the app's sharing capabilities.

Allow user to scan document QR code from UAE pass app to trigger peer to peer sharing request with another user. Allow QR code can be shared via available channels between users (Online and Face to face) Allow sharing request to be trigged for the needed document between users once QR is scanned for the specified document Allow user to give specific permission related to the shared document via QR (e.g., Specify if user can download document details or not)   This feature introduces QR code-based peer-to-peer document sharing for UAE PASS users. Users can scan a document’s QR code with the UAE PASS app to initiate a sharing request. The QR code can be shared through various channels, both online and face-to-face. Once scanned, a sharing request is triggered for the specified document. Users also have the ability to set specific permissions for the shared document, such as whether the document details can be downloaded or not. This ensures consistency in the behavior of QR codes within UAE PASS, enhancing the overall user experience   Increase awareness of DV users as document sharing feature is used frequency between people. Title: Generate Statistics on Optional Document Sharing in Sharing Requests   As a product manager I want the ops team to provide insights into the usage of optional documents in sharing requests So that I can analyze the effectiveness of optional document sharing and identify areas for improvement.   Acceptance Criteria: Data Insights:   Generate the following statistics for sharing requests that include optional documents: Percentage of sharing requests where at least one optional document was shared. Percentage of sharing requests where no optional documents were shared. Percentage of sharing requests with optional documents that failed. Accuracy:   Ensure the data includes all sharing requests for a specific period (to be defined, e.g., last quarter or last year). Categorize the statistics by service providers and document types for deeper analysis.

User Story: Usage Statistics for Predefined Document Requests Title: Analyze Utilization of Predefined Document Requests by Service Providers   As a product team we wish the ops team to analyze how often service providers utilize the predefined document request feature So that we can evaluate its adoption and identify opportunities for improvement or further enablement.   Acceptance Criteria: Data Insights:   Generate the following statistics: Percentage of total document requests where predefined document details (e.g., vehicle registration for a specific car number) are utilized. List of service providers that have used the predefined document request feature and their respective frequency of usage. Comparison of predefined document requests against generic requests for the same document types. Segmentation:   Provide breakdowns by: Document type (e.g., Vehicle Registration, marriage certificate). Service provider.

Acceptance Criteria: Data Insights:   Provide the following statistics: Percentage of one-to-many document requests configured to allow: Single selection only. Multiple selections. Cases where the same document is requested: As mandatory and optional in the same request. Segmentation:   Breakdown data by: Service provider. Document type. Request configuration (mandatory vs. optional).
